items.title: Ink TUI + streaming
items.summary: React/Ink 7 app — streaming transcript, tool activity, spinner, inline approvals, slash commands.
items.done: open argo → full TUI with streaming tokens.

items.title: Full REPL + install
items.summary: install.sh global argo command + full slash set.
items.done: argo works from anywhere.

items.title: Persistent model selection
items.summary: /model picker persists the choice to .env by default (^g = session-only). Selected model survives relaunch instead of reverting to the ollama/qwen seed.
items.done: Pick a model in the TUI → it's still the model next launch.

items.title: @-context references
items.summary: Composer @-autocomplete for files — extended (per the audit) to @file/@diff/@url context references (Hermes + Claude Code both have this). A cheap externalize-context affordance: less typing/recall to hand Argo context. ND fit F2+F4. Promoted from horizon by the audit.
items.done: Type @ in the TUI → autocomplete files/diff/url and inline them as context.

items.title: Calmer TUI (less firehose)
items.summary: tui/tool-display.ts (pure, 14 tests): toolDisplay(name,args)→{icon,verb,detail} computed at dispatch time — clean verbs + a per-tool verb map (read/wrote/ran/searched/saw screen/git·sub/…), abbrevPath (temp dirs incl NSIRD → basename, $HOME→~, deep→…/last/two), never raw JSON. Detail comes from ARGS not raw output (kills the temp-path leak; output→model only). On error a red firstLine tail stays visible. partitionBlocks groups consecutive tool rows into one indented cluster. transcript.tsx renders the parts purely; app.tsx reducer carries icon/verb/detail + errorLine. Design note: docs/tui-smooth-design.md. DEFERRED (needs Jason's interactive design): collapse/expand folding + the 'does it feel calm' judgment — Hashmark turned out to be a web app, not a transferable TUI reference.
items.done: A multi-tool turn reads as a calm grouped feed — no raw temp paths, no JSON dumps, indented clusters. Interactive collapse/expand flagged for brainstorming.

items.title: Gemini provider
items.summary: Google OpenAI-compatible endpoint, GEMINI_API_KEY.
items.done: ARGO_PROVIDER=gemini argo run returns on gemini-2.5-flash.

items.title: OpenRouter provider
items.summary: One key, 200+ models.
items.done: OPENROUTER_API_KEY works.

items.title: argo setup wizard
items.summary: Provider picker, hidden key prompt, merge into .env.
items.done: First-run wizard configures any backend.

items.title: First-run detection
items.summary: No backend on launch → auto-run argo setup.
items.done: Clean install self-configures.

items.title: argo status / doctor
items.summary: Boxed health: kernel ping, provider, key presence, counts.
items.done: argo status shows green health.

items.title: Claude subscription provider
items.summary: ARGO_PROVIDER=claude-code uses Claude Pro/Max OAuth token.
items.done: claude-code provider authenticates.

items.title: ChatGPT-Codex OAuth
items.summary: ARGO_PROVIDER=codex, Responses API, shared ~/.codex/auth.json.
items.done: Live-verified end-to-end.

items.title: Post-turn nudge counters
items.summary: shouldReview: busy turn or periodic interval triggers background review.
items.done: Review fires automatically.

items.title: Background-review fork
items.summary: Post-turn tool-restricted agent replays transcript, writes skills.
items.done: Live-verified: judged no skill on trivial turn.

items.title: Skill provenance + safe curator
items.summary: argo-learned tag, curator archives only learned-stale skills, 7d interval.
items.done: Curator runs at session start without breaking anything.

items.title: Dark factory (self-improving codebase)
items.summary: factory/ module: triage→plan→execute→verify→commit. argo improve + argo factory approve.
items.done: Live end-to-end: verifier correctly rejected bad model output.

items.title: Autonomy ladder (L1–L4, kernel-bounded)
items.summary: factory/run.ts now runs at a configurable autonomy level: L1 suggest (plan, no branch) · L2 implement (branch+execute+verify, stop for review) · L3 commit (no push) · L4 push. resolveAutonomyLevel(sub,env): improve=L1, approve=ARGO_AUTONOMY_LEVEL (default 4). Split commitAndPush→commitSlice+pushBranch; new CycleResult statuses implemented + committed{pushed}. Kernel is_protected_path still blocks skeleton/brainstem at every level. L5 auto-merge + low-risk classifier deferred (riskiest rung).
items.done: argo factory approve with ARGO_AUTONOMY_LEVEL=2|3|4 stops exactly at implement/commit/push; 15 run.test.ts incl. resolveAutonomyLevel.

items.title: Autonomy L5 (auto-merge low-risk)
items.summary: factory/merge.ts: assessMergeRisk fails-closed through 3 gates — armed (ARGO_AUTONOMY_ALLOW_MERGE, default OFF), safe target (resolveMergeTarget default factory/integration, never main/master, --no-ff never force), low-risk (limbs/reflexes/memory only via O11, no dep/env/config/migration file, ≤20 files & ≤400 diff lines). MAX_AUTONOMY_LEVEL 4→5. run.ts: after L4 push, an armed+low-risk slice merges into the integration branch (mergeIntoTarget restores HEAD, aborts on conflict/missing target → stays L4). The git lifecycle runs outside the kernel assess() gate, so the classifier is the whole safety story; it's pure + 11 merge.test.ts. Merge git sequence smoke-verified (clean + conflict-abort + missing-target).
items.done: A low-risk verified slice auto-merges when armed; anything failing the classifier stops at L4 push. Default OFF (riskiest rung — opt-in).

items.title: Compartmentalized self-repair (body model)
items.summary: factory/compartments.ts: classifyCompartment(file)→tier (skeleton/brainstem/limbs/reflexes/memory), compartmentMaxAutonomy(tier)→max L (skeleton 0 · brainstem 2 · limbs/reflexes/memory 5), autonomyCapForFiles(files)→most-restrictive cap. run.ts clamps the requested autonomy level to the cap of the files a slice ACTUALLY touched (mixed slice → minimum). A brainstem fix can't auto-commit even at L4; only limbs/reflexes/memory reach the full ladder. is_protected_path stays the hard boundary; this is the soft policy it can't express.
items.done: A tier map exists + the factory refuses to exceed a compartment's max autonomy: limb fixes flow autonomously, brainstem/skeleton don't. 12 compartments.test.ts.

items.title: Emergent self-designed brain
items.summary: Let Argo design its own brain substrate (its own format/code).
items.done: Open research — no fixed done line.

items.title: Session persist + resume
items.summary: File-based sessions, argo sessions list, argo resume <id>.
items.done: Resume rehydrates a prior conversation.

items.title: Port skills library
items.summary: 10 high-value skills ported, argo skills install.
items.done: 10/10 installed live.

items.title: Skill bundles
items.summary: YAML bundle schema for composite slash commands.
items.done: One /slash loads several skills.

items.title: Daemon / service mode
items.summary: argo gateway foreground daemon + launchd service manager.
items.done: Foreground daemon starts/ticks/stops.

items.title: Telegram gateway
items.summary: Long-poll + allowlist, wired into gateway.
items.done: Offline-tested; live needs bot token.

items.title: Webhook triggers
items.summary: HMAC-gated webhook server, resolveDeliver, background agent run.
items.done: Real localhost requests: 200 signed / 401 unsigned.

items.title: MCP client
items.summary: Dependency-free stdio JSON-RPC client + mount.ts. Mounted in prepareRun.
items.done: Protocol unit-tested; live needs a real server + config fix (MCP-1).

items.title: Writable zones beyond the repo
items.summary: write_file now writes outside the repo into bounded, approval-gated writable zones (default ~/Desktop+~/Downloads, ARGO_WRITABLE_DIRS override) instead of hard-refusing. TS-only: the kernel already returned Ask for out-of-root paths; the tool was stricter than the boundary. ~-expansion + prefix-collision-safe zone check. Out-of-zone still refused (backstop vs yes-fatigue).
items.done: Live-verified: write_file wrote directly to ~/Desktop in one step — no repo pollution, no shell copy.

items.title: Readable zones (read across the workspace)
items.summary: read_file hard-refused out-of-repo reads, so Argo couldn't read sibling-repo skills (e.g. ~/Documents/GitHub/theft-kit). Mirror of SCOPE-1: read_file now reads from readable zones — default = the project's PARENT dir (sibling repos in the same workspace) + the writable zones; ARGO_READABLE_DIRS override. Shared isInZone/expandHome helper. Follow-up: secret-filename read-guard (.env/keys) even in-zone.
items.done: Live-verified: read theft-kit/design-html/SKILL.md (64KB); ~/.ssh/id_rsa still refused.

items.title: Agent-chosen model on delegate
items.summary: delegate tool with provider/model params.
items.done: Agent routes subtasks to any backend.

items.title: Swarms
items.summary: tools/swarm.ts — parallel multi-agent fan-out + synthesize.
items.done: Swarm dispatches and returns.

items.done: Needs Screen Recording permission.

items.done: Shipped.

items.done: Shipped.

items.done: Shipped.

items.summary: Brain regions, brain tool, SOUL.md, AGENT-MANIFESTO.md.
items.done: Argo has a persistent identity.

items.title: Heartbeat selfhood updates
items.summary: Wire brain writes onto the gateway tick.
items.done: Identity evolves continuously via daemon.

items.title: Slash-command parity
items.summary: /history /retry /undo /reset /title /fork.
items.done: Full slash parity readline + TUI.

items.title: Interactive product roadmap
items.summary: roadmap.json (agent-ready) → roadmap.html (Now/Next/Later view). argo roadmap command.
items.done: argo roadmap opens the view; Argo reads roadmap.json natively.

items.title: Secret-hygiene hardening
items.summary: gitleaks pre-commit hook + .example twins, installed by install.sh.
items.done: A secret-shaped string can't be committed (verified: hook runs gitleaks protect --staged).

items.title: One-line curl install
items.summary: bootstrap.sh clones Argo into ~/argo (ARGO_DIR override, default branch — no pin) then runs install.sh. install.sh has no interactive prompts so piped installs can't crash (no /dev/tty probe needed). Documented in README.
items.done: Clone+install logic verified via local clone smoke; curl-pipe one-liner activates when the repo flips public.

items.title: Use any MCP (consume)
items.summary: readMcpConfig accepts mcpServers key + discovers ./.mcp.json (project) over ~/.argo/mcp.json (user). argo mcp list.
items.done: Config discovery shipped; live consume needs a real server.

items.title: Make + hook in at runtime
items.summary: mount_mcp tool (live runtime mount, kernel-gated) + build-mcp-server skill (scaffold on demand).
items.done: mount_mcp registers a server's tools into the live registry; skill teaches scaffolding.

items.title: Be a server (serve)
items.summary: argo mcp serve — exposes Argo tools over MCP stdio, kernel-gated + read-only allowlist. console.log→stderr keeps the protocol stream clean.
items.done: Live-verified: real McpClient drove argo mcp serve subprocess — handshake, tools/list, allow→execute, out-of-scope refused, non-allowlisted refused. Claude Code integration untested.

items.title: Prefer-local routing
items.summary: Auto-route simple work to local Ollama on M4 Pro.
items.done: Simple tasks dispatch to Ollama without manual ARGO_PROVIDER override.

items.title: Task-initiation affordance
items.summary: From the 4-way audit (see audit.html): the executive-function activation wall is the #1 ND need and NO surveyed agent (Hermes/Goose/Claude Code) targets it directly — Argo's true differentiator, not a copy. A 'start the next tiny step' affordance: given a goal, surface ONE concrete smallest next action and let the user begin with a single keystroke. ND fit F1 (initiation) + F5 (structure).
items.done: From a stated goal, Argo proposes one tiny next step and the user can start it in one action — measured: time-from-open-to-first-action drops.

items.title: clarify tool (ask before acting)
items.summary: Ported concept from Hermes clarify_tool.py. A tool that lets the agent ask ONE structured question before acting when intent is ambiguous, instead of guessing and thrashing. Kernel-gated like any tool. ND fit F1+F4+F6 (predictable, low-vigilance, ask-don't-assume). Small surface, high fit.
items.done: On ambiguous intent Argo asks one tappable structured question instead of guessing; wrong-guess rework drops.

items.title: Plan-first / converse mode
items.summary: Two agents converged here (Hermes 'converse mode' + Goose '/plan'). An opt-in mode where Argo clarifies → lays out the steps → confirms → then acts, instead of acting immediately. Breaks blank-page paralysis and gives a calm act-gate. Extends Argo's autonomy ladder L1 into an interactive front door. ND fit F1+F4+F6.
items.done: A /plan (or converse) mode produces a confirmed step list before any tool runs; toggleable; integrates with the autonomy ladder.

items.title: Top-of-mind reminder (MOIM)
items.summary: Ported from Goose 'tom'/MOIM. A short pinned 'what am I doing / don't forget X' note the user sets, injected into every turn's prompt as a volatile tier. Pure working-memory offload — smallest, highest-fit ND feature. ND fit F2+F5.
items.done: User pins a top-of-mind note; it appears in the prompt every turn until cleared; survives across a session.

items.title: Per-model capability matrix
items.summary: From the Claude Code survey (claude-code-haha modelSupportOverrides) + Goose issue #9529. A small declarative map of which optional params (thinking/reasoning_effort/etc.) a model accepts, so free-typed OpenRouter/Ollama model IDs don't get sent params they 400/500 on. Lives alongside providers/catalog.ts. ND fit F6 (reliability removes vigilance burden).
items.done: Free-typing an OpenRouter/Ollama model that lacks thinking/effort no longer errors — Argo omits unsupported params per a capability lookup.

items.title: Proactive nudge cadence
items.summary: Hermes users hacked ADHD morning/evening standups + unprompted reminders on top of cron; make it native and GENTLE (respect F3 — a nudge, never a nag). Optional scheduled gentle check-ins tied to goals ('still on X? want the next step?'). ND fit F1+F5.
items.done: User opts into a gentle cadence; Argo sends a calm goal-aware check-in on schedule via the gateway; easy to snooze/stop.

items.title: Voice conversational loop
items.summary: Hermes voice_mode + an explicit accessibility demand in its user-stories ('can't type well, voice is huge'; a blind user built an NVDA addon). Argo already has speak/transcribe tools; the gap is a live mic-in → spoken-reply loop. ND fit F1 (initiation, lower input friction) + F3 (calm). Broad ND / motor accessibility.
items.done: A voice mode runs a live transcribe→agent→speak loop in the terminal; degrades gracefully without mic/STT.

items.title: Checkpoints + /rollback
items.summary: Ported concept from Hermes checkpoint_manager.py. Shadow-git auto-snapshot before destructive ops + a /rollback to restore. Argo's kernel BLOCKS risky ops but doesn't snapshot/restore — an undo safety net lowers anxiety around letting the agent act. ND fit F6 (trust). Opt-in, dedup store.
items.done: Argo snapshots before a destructive op; /rollback restores the prior state; never touches kernel-protected paths.

items.title: Typed stream-event contract
items.summary: Ported concept from the local-Hermes customization (stream_events.py + stream_dispatch.py): a typed event vocabulary (message chunk/stop, commentary, tool-call chunk/finished, long-tool hint, notice) that names WHAT happened, and a per-surface dispatcher where a surface that can't render tool chrome can 'eat' it. Generalizes TUI-SMOOTH's calm feed beyond the TUI (telegram/webhook). ND fit F3 (calm output everywhere).
items.done: Agent emits typed events; each surface (TUI/telegram/webhook) renders or suppresses per its capability; the TUI feed is driven by the typed contract.

items.title: Recovery / degraded CLI
items.summary: From the Claude Code survey (claude-code-haha localRecoveryCli): a plain readline fallback behind a flag for when Ink can't render (CI, bad TERM, native-dep failure). Argo's readline REPL partly covers this; formalize it as a guaranteed degraded mode. ND fit F6 (reliability — always reachable).
items.done: ARGO_FORCE_RECOVERY_CLI (or auto-detect) runs a minimal readline agent loop when the Ink TUI can't start.

items.title: Live roadmap kanban (you + Argo control) + WIP limit
items.summary: The roadmap IS already a kanban (status enum = columns; render.ts renders Now/Next/Later). Make it bi-directionally LIVE, source of truth stays roadmap.json (agent-ready, git-versioned). (1) Argo control: roadmap_move tool + `argo roadmap move <id> <status>` (kernel-gated write→regenerate); the dark factory moves cards next→building on start, building→shipped on verified-commit. (2) Human control: roadmap.html drag-to-move POSTs to a tiny local endpoint (argo roadmap serve / kernel /api/roadmap/move) that rewrites roadmap.json. (3) THE EF FEATURE — a hard WIP limit on Now(building)=1-2: moving a 3rd card in refuses and asks you to finish or park one. WIP limit = the anti-bounce/finish-what-you-start prosthesis (targets the documented ideas-rich/finish-poor pattern + this session’s live 8-goal bounce). Ship #1 first (smallest slice). Research: docs/kanban-roadmap-research.md. ND fit F2+F4+F5+F6.
items.done: Both you and Argo move cards on one live board (roadmap.json source); Now is WIP-limited and refuses a 3rd card until one is finished or parked.

items.title: Dynamic workflows (agent writes its own orchestration harness)
items.summary: From Anthropic’s Claude Code team (Thariq Shihipar & Sid Bidasaria, “A harness for every task”, /Desktop PDF). Let Argo write its own JS orchestration harness on the fly to spawn+coordinate sub-agents, extending the existing delegate+swarm. Canonical patterns: classify-and-act, fan-out-and-synthesize, adversarial-verification, generate-and-filter, tournament, loop-until-done. Combats the long-context failure modes the article names — agentic laziness (stops at 20/50 items), self-preferential bias (prefers own results when judging), goal drift (loses the objective after compaction) — by giving each sub-agent its own clean context + isolated goal. Token budgets (“use 10k tokens”), worktree isolation, model/intelligence routing per subtask, save/reuse workflows. VISION TIE-IN: this is executive function FOR THE AGENT — making Argo itself finish and stay on-objective — the mirror of the human-EF moat. Pairs with Argo’s /goal + a /loop equivalent.
items.done: Argo can compose+run a multi-agent workflow (fan-out/synthesize, adversarial-verify, tournament, loop-until-done) with a token budget; a long/parallel task that single-context drifts on completes without agentic laziness or goal drift.

items.title: Safe kernel self-repair (propose→prove→swap)
items.summary: Design: docs/self-repair-architecture.md. Limbs already self-heal (O11+factory); this closes the skeleton/kernel tier WITHOUT letting the running boundary edit itself in place. (1) propose-only candidate kernel binary, never overwrite live; (2) differential safety-invariant conformance — the OLD kernel judges the NEW, reject if more permissive on any Rule-Zero case; (3) blue-green shadow swap + auto-rollback to last-good (never breaks the system); (4) human promotion for any safety-contract diff, classifier FAILS CLOSED; (5) immutable trust root = the promotion authority + invariant spec (machine-checkable twin of MANIFESTO Rule Zero) — the one thing Argo cannot self-modify. ND fit F6 (an agent that maintains itself removes the vigilance burden). HORIZON — kernel blast radius; kernel stays hard-blocked until built.
items.done: A non-weakening kernel change promotes via conformance + blue-green with auto-rollback; any safety-contract change requires human promotion; the live boundary is never edited in place.

items.title: Born-small codegen (registry-by-default + 300 gate)
items.summary: Make the dark factory GENERATE small, independently-repairable units by default (the broken-leg property without later refactoring). Doc: docs/factory-evolution.md. 3 human-authored slices on factory/*.ts (skeleton): (1) verifier HARD GATE — pure checkNewFilesUnderLineLimit on NEW source files only (<=300; new-files-only is universally safe vs false-positiving bug fixes to pre-existing large files) + argo-ts/CONVENTIONS.md (3-tier taxonomy: HARD GATE/GUIDANCE/CONVENTION DOC + the Tools-registry template); (2) planner gene-transfusion — prepend a PROVEN-PATTERNS block (spec wins on conflict) to buildInstruction for roadmap+parked only; (3) executor wiring — inject per-dir CLAUDE.md from plan.touchedDirs (closes the loop: factory requires those docs but never reads them back). Canonical template = tools/index.ts ALL_TOOLS + buildRegistry; mirrored by repl/handlers HANDLERS.
items.done: Factory refuses to birth a >300-line unit, instructs new-feature work to land as a registry unit (new file + one entry, no switch edit), and feeds each dir's own CLAUDE.md back to the agent.

items.title: Holdout author-separation validation
items.summary: From OctopusGarden + StrongDM + Willison (triple-confirmed). The codegen agent must NOT author its own acceptance check — today the same cycle writes code + test, so it can write a weak test (self-preferential-bias / reward-hacking). Hold a scenario/acceptance set the executor never sees; a separate implementation-blind agent (or pre-existing scenario) judges it. This is Argo's SECOND validation layer (the kernel is the first) — NOT a kernel replacement. Adapt the idea, not the Digital-Twin-Universe machinery (irrelevant to self-improvement).
items.done: A factory slice is accepted only against criteria the implementing agent never saw; a slice that games its own test is caught.

items.title: Intent-satisfaction LLM-judge gate
items.summary: The biggest concrete gap found in the audit: the verifier proves code is CORRECT (tests pass, tsc clean) but never that it DID WHAT THE ITEM ASKED — a slice can pass every test and not fulfill the work item. Add an LLM-as-judge gate scoring intent-satisfaction against the work item; keep BOOLEAN for the deterministic checks (boolean is correct for did-the-build-break — do NOT fuzz those), judged only for subjective intent.
items.done: A slice that passes tests but doesn't address the work item's intent is rejected by the intent judge; deterministic gates stay boolean.

items.title: Ambiguity-gated preflight (unifies with ND clarify)
items.summary: From Ouroboros (Ambiguity = 1 - sum(clarity*weight), gate before codegen) — take the lightweight GATE, not the 9-minds/Double-Diamond ceremony. Before running a cycle, assess whether the work item is clear enough; if not, clarify or skip rather than burn tokens + drift. One mechanism serving BOTH the factory preflight AND the ND ND2 clarify / ND3 plan-first items.
items.done: The factory refuses to execute a too-vague item and routes it to clarify; clear items proceed. Shared with the ND clarify/plan-first surface.

items.title: Model escalation + per-cycle cost ledger
items.summary: Answers Willison's ~$1k/day cost caution and serves the frugality drive. Start a cycle on a cheap/local model; escalate to a stronger model after N non-improving iterations, demote after improvements (OctopusGarden pattern). Add per-cycle cost/token accounting (Argo has budgets but no escalation or cost ledger). Ties to routing/model-router + delegate.
items.done: A factory cycle starts frugal, escalates only when stalled, and records per-cycle token/cost; cheap work never pays premium-model cost.

items.title: Stall recovery / bounded-retry
items.summary: Today a verify-fail discards the slice with no recovery. Adapt Ouroboros stagnation detection (spinning/oscillation/no-drift/diminishing-returns, bounded <=N iterations) + OctopusGarden wonder->reflect (high-temp diagnose -> surgical fix). Replace single-shot discard with bounded iterate + a strategy switch on stall.
items.done: A stalled cycle detects the stall pattern and retries with a changed strategy within a bounded budget instead of discarding immediately.

items.title: Work-item closure loop
items.summary: When the factory ships a work item it should CLOSE it — tick the ROADMAP/PARKED checkbox or move the KANBAN card to shipped — not just commit code. Closes the triage->done loop so the backlog reflects reality automatically. Ties directly to the KANBAN item (Argo moving cards as it works).
items.done: A shipped factory slice automatically closes/moves its source work item; the board reflects what was actually shipped without manual edits.

items.title: Composer basics: input history + multiline
items.summary: Argo's composer is single-line and explicitly ignores up/down (composer.tsx line 75). All four references have these table-stakes: (1) input HISTORY — up/down recalls prior messages (Gemini, Claude Code); (2) MULTILINE input — shift+enter / backslash / paste a block (all); (3) graceful large-paste (paste a big block → collapse to a reference rather than flooding the line). The #1 'missing normal stuff'. ND fit F1 (lower input friction).
items.done: Up/down recalls history; shift+enter inserts a newline; pasting a large block doesn't break the line.

items.title: Esc to interrupt a running turn
items.summary: Claude Code / Codex / Gemini all let Esc cancel a running turn. Argo HAS the mechanism (createConversation signal + AbortController, stoppedReason 'interrupted') but the TUI never wires a key to it — you can't stop a runaway turn from the Ink UI (only the one-shot CLI has SIGINT). Wire Esc → controller.abort() in app.tsx. ND fit F6 (control/trust) + F3 (stop the firehose).
items.done: Pressing Esc during a busy turn aborts it cleanly between iterations and returns control to the composer.

items.title: Markdown + syntax-highlight rendering
items.summary: Assistant output renders as plain <Text> today. Gemini + Claude Code render markdown (headings/lists/bold/links) + syntax-highlighted code blocks. For an ND-readability product this is core F3 (calm, scannable output, not a wall of monospace) — arguably Argo's highest-value TUI gap given the vision. Use an Ink markdown renderer + a highlighter; respect the calm aesthetic (no neon).
items.done: Assistant messages render markdown structure + highlighted code blocks; output is scannable, not a flat wall of text.

items.title: In-TUI mode / approval toggle (shift+tab)
items.summary: Claude Code cycles normal/auto-accept/plan with shift+tab; Codex has /approvals + mode switch; Gemini has a yolo toggle. Argo gates via the kernel + autonomy ladder (L1-5) but offers no in-session toggle — you can't flip 'ask me every time' vs 'just go' without env vars. Map a shift+tab cycle onto the autonomy ladder / approval posture (kernel still the hard floor). ND fit F4 (one control for how much it asks).
items.done: A visible mode indicator cycles via shift+tab (e.g. review / auto within kernel bounds); the kernel remains the hard floor.

items.title: Diff rendering for file edits
items.summary: Codex + Claude Code render a colored diff when the agent edits a file, before/at approval. Argo's write_file shows a clean tool line (post TUI-SMOOTH) but no diff — you can't see WHAT changed in the TUI. Render a unified diff for write_file/edits in the activity feed + the approval prompt. ND fit F6 (see exactly what it did → trust).
items.done: A file edit shows a concise colored diff in the feed and in its approval prompt.

items.title: Prefix shortcuts: bash (!) + memory (#)
items.summary: Claude Code: '!' runs a shell command inline (output into context), '#' appends a line to memory without a full command. Argo has shell_cmd as a tool + /memory as a command but no fast prefixes. Add composer prefixes: leading ! → shell (kernel-gated), leading # → brain/memory append. ND fit F1 (lower friction for the two most common quick actions).
items.done: Typing '! <cmd>' runs a kernel-gated shell command; '# <note>' appends to the brain — no slash command needed.

items.title: Custom user slash commands
items.summary: Claude Code + Gemini let users define their own slash commands (markdown/prompt files in a commands dir). Argo's slash set is a fixed registry (repl/handlers.ts). Add user-defined commands discovered from ~/.argo/commands/ (or skills) that expand into a prompt — extend the HANDLERS registry at load. ND fit F1/F5 (capture a repeated workflow as one keystroke).
items.done: A user drops a command file in ~/.argo/commands/; it appears in the palette and runs as a templated prompt.

items.title: Persistent rich status line
items.summary: Gemini + Claude Code show a persistent status line (model · context % · tokens · sometimes cost). Argo has a one-line hint + /context + /usage commands but no always-on status. Add a calm persistent footer: model, context-fill %, token/cost (ties to FAC-ESCALATE cost ledger). ND fit F2/F6 (ambient state without asking).
items.done: A persistent footer shows model + context% + tokens; updates live, stays calm (no flicker).

items.title: Themes incl. accessibility (high-contrast / dyslexia)
items.summary: Gemini ships selectable themes. For an ND product this is accessibility, not decoration: a high-contrast theme, a low-arousal muted theme, and a dyslexia-friendly option (spacing/weight). Token-drive the TUI colors + ARGO_THEME. ND fit F3 (sensory regulation — let the user tune arousal/contrast).
items.done: ARGO_THEME selects among calm / high-contrast / dyslexia-friendly palettes; persists like the model choice.

items.title: Thinking / reasoning display (collapsible)
items.summary: Claude Code shows (collapsible) thinking; Codex exposes reasoning levels. Argo's loop discards/ignores reasoning in the TUI. When a provider returns reasoning, show it dim + collapsed (expand on key) so the user can see WHY without the firehose. ND fit F3 (optional depth, calm by default) + F6 (transparency).
items.done: Reasoning, when present, shows as a dim collapsed line that expands on demand; off by default to stay calm.

items.title: Vim-mode composer editing
items.summary: Gemini + Claude Code offer vim-mode line editing. Lower priority but cheap given the custom composer already owns key handling (composer.tsx). Add an opt-in modal (normal/insert) editing mode behind ARGO_VIM. ND fit F1 (muscle-memory for vim users = lower friction).
items.done: ARGO_VIM enables modal editing in the composer (normal/insert, basic motions); off by default.

items.title: Keyboard shortcut help overlay
items.summary: Most TUIs offer a '?' / shortcut to a key-binding cheat sheet. Argo has /help (commands) but no key-binding reference and the custom composer keys (ctrl a/e/u/k/w, etc.) are undiscoverable. Add a '?' overlay listing keys + modes. ND fit F2 (don't make me remember the bindings).
items.done: A '?' overlay lists every composer key, slash command, and mode toggle; dismiss with esc.
